definition: Relating to the heart and blood vessels.
example: Running improves cardiovascular fitness.
usageNote: Cardiovascular exercise raises heart rate safely when paced gradually.

definition: One tiny dot of color on a screen; millions make an image.
example: That icon looks blurry when pixels are stretched.
usageNote: Higher pixel counts usually mean sharper displays, but viewing distance matters too.

definition: An animal that hunts other animals for food.
example: The shark is an apex predator in its reef habitat.
usageNote: Predators help control prey populations and keep ecosystems balanced.

definition: A chemical change microbes cause in food, like bread rising.
example: Yogurt forms through fermentation of milk.
etymology: From Latin fermentum — leaven or yeast.
usageNote: Fermentation preserves food and creates tangy flavors in pickles and kimchi.

definition: Mass in motion; the tendency to keep moving or growing.
example: Early goals gave the team momentum.
usageNote: In physics and sports, momentum describes both literal motion and psychological streaks.

definition: All living things and their environment interacting as one system.
example: Coral reefs are fragile ecosystems sensitive to warming water.
usageNote: Removing one species can ripple through an entire ecosystem.

definition: To find and fix errors in computer code.
example: We debugged the app until the login button worked.
usageNote: Systematic debugging — reproduce, isolate, fix — beats random guessing.

definition: Requiring oxygen; exercise that raises breathing and heart rate.
example: Swimming is an aerobic workout for the whole body.
usageNote: Aerobic activity builds endurance; anaerobic bursts build power.

definition: A protein in wheat that helps bread rise and stay chewy.
example: Some bakeries offer gluten-free rolls for celiac customers.
usageNote: Gluten gives dough elasticity; over-mixing can make it tough.

definition: Seasonal movement of animals from one region to another.
example: Geese begin migration south before the first frost.
etymology: From Latin migrare — to move from place to place.
usageNote: Migration follows food, weather, or breeding cycles — not random travel.

definition: Scrambling data so only authorized readers can decode it.
example: Encryption protects messages sent over public Wi-Fi.
usageNote: HTTPS uses encryption between your browser and websites.

definition: A rule violation when an attacking player is ahead of the ball too early.
example: The striker was called offside on the breakaway.
usageNote: Offside rules differ slightly between soccer and hockey — context matters.

definition: How plants use sunlight to turn carbon dioxide and water into sugar.
example: Photosynthesis is why leaves are green — chlorophyll captures light.
usageNote: Photosynthesis releases oxygen as a byproduct — essential for animal life.

definition: An animal that eats only plants.
example: Deer are herbivores that graze on grass and shrubs.

definition: A savory taste found in broth, cheese, and soy sauce.
example: Mushrooms add umami depth to vegetarian soup.
usageNote: Umami is the fifth basic taste alongside sweet, sour, salty, and bitter.
